units.sanskrit: अचिुष्ट्व न्न दृवष्टमे तथ ऽश्रोत्रस्य ि श्रुततः । अव क्त्व न्न तु वक्क्तः स्य िमन्स्त्व न्मततः ि ु तः ॥१॥
units.translation: There is no vision in Me as I am without the organ of seeing. How can there be hearing in Me who have no auditive organ? Devoid of the organ of speech I have no action of speaking in Me. How can there be thinking in Me who have no mind?

units.sanskrit: अप्र णस्य न िम यञ्स्त बुद्ध्यभ वे न वेदित । ववद्य ववद्ये ततो न स्तञ्श्चन्म त्रज्योततषो मम॥२॥ तनत्यमुक्तस्य शुद्धस्य ि ू टस्थस्य ववच सिनः। अमृतस्य ििस्यैवमशिीिस्य िवयि ॥३॥
units.translation: Devoid of the vital force I have no action (in Me), and devoid of the intellect I am not a knower. Ever free, ever pure, changeless, immovable, immortal, imperishable and bodiless I have no knowledge or ignorance in Me who am of the nature of the Light of Pure Consciousness only.

units.sanskrit: अप्र णस्य न िम यञ्स्त बुद्ध्यभ वे न वेदित । ववद्य ववद्ये ततो न स्तञ्श्चन्म त्रज्योततषो मम॥२॥ तनत्यमुक्तस्य शुद्धस्य ि ू टस्थस्य ववच सिनः। अमृतस्य ििस्यैवमशिीिस्य िवयि ॥३॥
units.translation: Devoid of the vital force I have no action (in Me), and devoid of the intellect I am not a knower. Ever free, ever pure, changeless, immovable, immortal, imperishable and bodiless I have no knowledge or ignorance in Me who am of the nature of the Light of Pure Consciousness only.

units.sanskrit: ञ्जर्त्ि व वपप ि व शोिमोहौ जि मृती । न ववद्यन्तेऽशिीित्व द्ध व्योमवद्धव्य वपनो मम ॥४॥
units.translation: All-pervading like the ether I have no hunger, thirst, grief, delusion, old age or death as I am without a body (mind and vital force).

units.sanskrit: अस्पशयत्व न्न मे स्पृवष्टन यञ्जह्वत्व द्रिज्ञत । तनत्यववज्ञ नरूपस्य ज्ञ न ज्ञ ने न मे िि ॥५॥
units.translation: Devoid of the organ of touch I have no act of touching; and devoid of the tongue I have no sensation of taste. I never have knowledge or ignorance as I am of the nature of eternal Consciousness.

units.sanskrit: य तु स्य न्म निी वृवत्तश्च िुष्ि रूपिचजन । तनत्यमेव त्मनो दृष्ट्य तनत्यय दृश्यते दह ि ॥६॥
units.translation: It is well known that the mental modification produced through the instrumentality of the eye and of the form of the object of vision, is always witnessed by the eternal Consciousness of the Self.

units.sanskrit: तथ न्येञ्न्द्रययुक्त य वृत्तयो ववशय चजन ः । स्मृती ि ग दिरूप च िे वि न्तमयनस्यवप ॥७॥ म नस्यस्तद्विन्यस्य दृश्यन्ते स्वप्नवृत्तयः । द्रुष्ट ु दृयवष्टस्ततो तनत्य शुद्ध नन्त च िे वि ॥८॥
units.translation: Similarly, other mental modifications in the forms of objects of knowledge produced through the instrumentality of other organs and also those in the forms of memory, attachment, etc. existing only within the mind, and those again in dream are witnessed by one different from all of them (i.e., by the Self). The Knowledge, therefore, of the Knower is eternal, pure, infinite and without a second.

units.sanskrit: तथ न्येञ्न्द्रययुक्त य वृत्तयो ववशय चजन ः । स्मृती ि ग दिरूप च िे वि न्तमयनस्यवप ॥७॥ म नस्यस्तद्विन्यस्य दृश्यन्ते स्वप्नवृत्तयः । द्रुष्ट ु दृयवष्टस्ततो तनत्य शुद्ध नन्त च िे वि ॥८॥
units.translation: Similarly, other mental modifications in the forms of objects of knowledge produced through the instrumentality of other organs and also those in the forms of memory, attachment, etc. existing only within the mind, and those again in dream are witnessed by one different from all of them (i.e., by the Self). The Knowledge, therefore, of the Knower is eternal, pure, infinite and without a second.

units.sanskrit: अतनत्य ि ऽववशुद्धेतत गृह्यतेऽत्र वववेितः । िुखी िुःखी तथ च हं दृश्ययोप चधभूतय ॥९॥
units.translation: It is through the indiscrimination between the Self and the modifications of the mind—knowable adjuncts to the Self—that the Knowledge of the Knower is wrongly conceived by the people to be impure and transitory, and the Self happy or miserable.

units.sanskrit: मूढय मूढ इत्येवं शुद्धय शुद्ध इत्यवप। | मन्यते िवयिोिोऽयं येन िंि िमृच्छतत॥१०॥
units.translation: All men misconceive themselves to be ignorant or pure according as they identify themselves with the mental modification, ‘I am ignorant’ or ‘I am pure’. It is for this reason that they continue to be in transmigratory existence.

units.sanskrit: अचिुष्ि दिश स्त्रोक्तं िब ह्य भ्यन्तिं त्वजम ् । तनत्यमुक्तसमह त्म नं मुमुिुश्चेत्िि स्मिेत ् ॥११॥
units.translation: One should always remember the Self to be ever-free, unborn and comprising the interior and exterior as described in the Sruti in which the Self is spoken of as ‘eyeless’ and so on if one is an aspirant after liberation.

units.sanskrit: अचिुष्ि दिश स्त्र च्च नेञ्न्द्रय णण िि मम । अप्र णो ह्यमन ः शुभ्र इतत च थवयणेवयचः ॥१२॥
units.translation: That organs never belong to me is known from the Sruti, ‘eyeless’ etc. There is again the saying of the Sruti belonging to the Atharva Veda that the Self is ‘devoid of the vital force, devoid of the mind and pure.’

units.sanskrit: शब्ि िीन मभ वश्च श्रूयते मम ि ठिे । अप्र णो ह्यमन यस्म िववि िी िि ह्यहम ् ॥१३॥
units.translation: As I am always devoid of the vital force and the mind and heard of in the Kaṭhopaniṣad as having no connection with sound etc. I am always changeless.

units.sanskrit: वविेपो न ञ्स्त तस्म न्मे न िम चधस्ततो मम । वविेपो व िम चधव य मनिः स्य दद्वि रिणः ॥१४॥
units.translation: I, therefore, have neither unrestfulness nor a profound concentration. Both of them belong to the mind which is subject to change.

units.sanskrit: अमनस्िस्य शुद्धस्य िथं तत्स्य द्ध द्वयं मम । अमनस्त्व ववि रित्वे वविेहव्य वपनो मम ॥१५॥
units.translation: How can I who am pure and mindless have those two? I am without any change and without a mind as I am all-pervading and devoid of a body.

units.sanskrit: इत्येतद्य विज्ञ नं त वत्ि यं मम भवत ् । तनत्यमुक्तस्य शुद्धस्य बुद्धस्य च िि मम ॥१६॥
units.translation: So, I who am ever free, ever pure and ever awakened had duties to perform so long as there was Ignorance.

units.sanskrit: िम चधव यऽिम चधव य ि यं च न्यत्ि ु तो भवेत ् । म ं दह ्य त्व च बुद्ध्व च मन्यन्ते ि ृ ति ृ त्यत म ् ॥१७॥
units.translation: How can I have concentration, non-concentration or other actions in Me as all men feel that the acme of their lives is fulfilled when they meditate on Me and know Me?

units.sanskrit: अहं ब्रह्म ञ्स्म िवोऽञ्स्म शुद्धो बुद्धोऽस्वयतः िि । अजः िवयत एव हमजिश्च ियोऽमृतः॥१८॥
units.translation: I am, therefore, Brahman, the all-comprehensive Principle, ever Pure, ever Awakened and ever Unborn, devoid of old age, imperishable and immortal.

units.sanskrit: मिन्यः िवयभूतेषु बोद्ध िञ्श्चन्न ववद्यते । िम य्यिश्च ि िी च चेत तनत्योऽगुणोऽद्वयः ॥१९॥
units.translation: There is no knower other than Myself among all the beings. I am the distributor of the results of their actions and the witness. It is I to whom all beings owe their consciousness. Without qualities and without a second I am eternal.

units.sanskrit: न िच्च हं न च िच्च नोभयं िे विः सशवः । न मे िं्य न ि बत्रव य न हव य िवयि दृशेः ॥२०॥
units.translation: I am not the three visible elements nor the two invisible ones, neither am I both (i.e., their combination, the body). I am devoid of all attributes and the three Gunas. In Me there is neither night nor day nor their juncture as I am always of the nature of light.

units.sanskrit: िवयमूततयववयुक्त ं यद्यथ खं िूक्ष्ममद्वयम ् । तेन प्यञ्स्म ववन भूतं ब्रह्मैव हं तथ ऽद्वयम ् ॥२१॥
units.translation: Just as the ether is subtle, without a second and devoid of all forms, so, I am the non-dual Brahman devoid even of the ether.

units.sanskrit: मम त्म स्वत आत्मेतत भेिो व्योवनो यथ भवेत ् 1। एिस्य िुवषभेिेन तथ मम वविञ्ल्पतः॥२२॥
units.translation: The distinction between the Self in Itself and my Self is due to the superimposition (of different adjuncts on one and the same Self), just as difference (is wrongly conceived to) exist in one and the same ether owing to apertures (in various objects).

units.sanskrit: भेिोऽभेिस्तथ चैिो न न चेतत वविञ्ल्पतः । ज्ञेयं ज्ञ त गततगयन्त म्येिञ्स्मन्ि ु तो भवेत ् ॥२३॥
units.translation: How can difference, absence of difference, oneness, manyness and the qualities of being known and being a knower, the results of actions and also agency and experiencing be attributed to Me who am one only?

units.sanskrit: न मे हेयं न च िेयमववि िी यतो ह्यहम ् । िि मुक्तस्तथ शुद्धः िि बुद्धोऽगुणोऽद्वयः ॥२४॥
units.translation: I have nothing to reject or accept inasmuch as I am changeless. Always free, pure, awakened and without qualities, I am without a second.

units.sanskrit: इत्येवं िवयि त्म नं ववद्य त्िवं िम दहतः । ववदित्व म ं स्विेहस्थमृवषमुयक्तो ध्रुवो भवेत ् ॥२५॥
units.translation: One should, with great concentration of mind, always know the Self to be All. One certainly becomes all-knowing and free when one knows Me to be residing is one’s own body.

units.sanskrit: ि ृ ति ृ त्यश्च सिद्धश्च योगी ब्र ह्मण एव च । य एवं वेि तत्त्व थयमन्यथ ह्य त्मह भवेत ् ॥२६॥
units.translation: He who thus knows the reality of the Self becomes successful in attaining the goal of his life and becomes perfect. He becomes a Knower of Brahman and one with It. One knowing the Self otherwise may be said to commit suicide.

units.sanskrit: वेि थो तनञ्श्चतो ह्येष िम िेन मयोदितः । िंन्य सिभ्यः प्रवक्तव्यः श न्तेभ्यः सशष्टबुवद्धन ॥२७॥
units.translation: This ascertained meaning of the Vedas described briefly by me should be imparted to those who have given up worldly action and controlled their minds by one whose intellect has been trained (according to the scriptures under a teacher who has known Brahman).
